intent: Build an osu!catch-style browser game where dropped files fall from the top and the user catches them with a basket. Caught files are either zipped and downloaded (client mode) or uploaded to a NestJS backend (server mode).
scope: bucket_catch/ — new top-level app directory in testsAndMisc
pnpm workspace with two packages: frontend (React 19 + Vite 6) and backend (NestJS 11)
No changes to existing Python packages or linux_configuration
changes: Created bucket_catch/packages/frontend: React 19 + Vite 6 app with DropZone, ModeSelect, GameCanvas, ScoreScreen components
Game engine: Canvas 2D requestAnimationFrame loop with AABB collision between falling file cards and mouse-tracked basket
Created bucket_catch/packages/backend: NestJS 11 app with POST /files/upload (multer disk storage) and GET /health
JSZip integration for local download mode; fetch-based upload for server mode
pnpm workspace root with parallel dev/build scripts

verification.evidence: 46 modules transformed, dist/assets/index.js 304 KB, built in 772ms, no TypeScript errors

risks: esbuild postinstall script is blocked by pnpm v10 approval system — binary exists but may need `pnpm rebuild esbuild` on a fresh install
Backend stores files in uploads/ on disk with no cleanup; directory will grow unbounded
rollback: rm -rf bucket_catch/ — entire feature is self-contained, no changes to existing packages
No database or system config changes to revert
